text:
There once was a little girl named Rosie. She lived in a small blue town on the coast where the sand was pink and the sea whispered secrets. Every day after school, Rosie would race home past sea birds singing their evening songs and pine trees swaying in the gentle breeze.

On Monday mornings, she liked to watch the fisherman casting their lines. By Wednesday, the seagulls were already clamoring for their fishy treats. And on Friday, Rosie's favorite place to people-watch was the waterfront market.

It was Friday afternoon when Rosie approached the fishmonger. "How's everything going?" she asked.

"One fish out of ten this week is pretty good," the fishmonger chuckled. "A strong winter storm hit last night. Got any fresh."

"I think so!" Rosie exclaimed. "Look!" She turned away from him, eyes widening.

A great salmon glided gracefully through the stream right beside the market stall. Rosie picked it up. "You'll have to come
